elements.algorithm-2411-001: [H] \caption{Solve $u$ by simulating Bessel processes in VSM} \label{gmm} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Require{$n$ is the number of capitalization processes we consider in the market, $m$ is the dimension of the time changed Bessel processes to model $X_i(t)$ for $i = 1, \ldots, n$. $N_T$ is the number of uniform meshes on $[0,T]$, $n_p$ is the sample trajectories used for Monte-Carlo simulation of $u(\cdot)$.} \For{$s \gets 0$ to $N_T$} \State {Initialize the states $\mathcal{X}(s) := (x_1, \ldots, x_n)$, $\theta_0 = s$. } \State {$k \gets 0$} \While{$\theta_k \leq T$} \State {$k \gets k+1$, $t_k := s + k \Delta t$.} \State {Generate $n_p$ samples of $m$-dimensional independent Brownian Motion $W(t_k)$} \State Simulate $n_p$ independent, $m$-dimensional squared Bessel processes $Y_i(t_{k}) = x_i + \big(\sum_{\ell = 1}^k W(t_{\ell}) \big)^2)$ and $mn$-dimensional squared Bessel processes $Y(t_{k}) = \sum_{i = 1}^n Y_i(t_{k})$. \State $\theta_{k+1} = \theta_k + \frac{4}{ Y(t_{k})} \Delta t$, where $\Delta t := T/N_T$. \EndWhile \State Collect $\{\theta_0, \ldots, \theta_{k+1}\}$; Simulate the squared Bessel bridge $R^b_{X(\theta_k), X(\theta_{k+1})}(T)$. Evaluate $X_{i}(T)$ by interpolation techniques between non-uniform mesh points $(\theta_k, \theta_{k+1})$. \State Compute $u(T-s, \mathcal{X}_s)$ via \eqref{eq:mc-u}. \EndFor \State \Return{The optimal arbitrage path $u(T-t, \mathcal{X}(t))$ for $t:= s \Delta t$, $s = 0, 1, \ldots, N_T$.} \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-002: [H] \caption{Solve $u$ by simulating Bessel processes in VSM} \label{gmm} Input: $n = $ the number of capitalization processes in the market, $m =$ the dimension of the time-changed Bessel processes to model $X_i(t)$ for $i = 1, \ldots, n$, $N_T = $the number of uniform meshes on $[0,T]$, $n_p =$ sample trajectories used for Monte Carlo simulation of $u(\cdot)$. For $s = 0$ to $N_T$: \begin{enumerate} \item Initialize $k = 0$, the states $\mathcal{X}(s) := (x_1, \ldots, x_n)$, $\theta_0 = s$. \item While $\theta_k \leq T$: \begin{enumerate} \item Set $k \gets k+1$, $t_k := s + k \Delta t$. \item Generate $n_p$ samples of $m$-dimensional independent Brownian Motion $W(t_k)$. \item Simulate $n_p$ independent samples of $mn$-dimensional squared Bessel processes $Y(t_{k}) = \sum_{i=1}^n Y_i(t_{k}),$ where $Y_i(t_{k})$ is defined in \eqref{eq:yi} for $i = 1, \ldots, n$. \item Update $\theta_{k+1} = \theta_k + \frac{4}{Y(t_{k})} \Delta t$, where $\Delta t := T / N_T$. \end{enumerate} \item Evaluate $X_{i}(T)$ using interpolation techniques between the non-uniform mesh points $(\theta_k, \theta_{k+1})$. Then, compute $u(T-s, \mathcal{X}_s)$ by the Monte Carlo estimation \eqref{eq:mc-u}. \end{enumerate} Output: The optimal arbitrage path $u(T-t, \mathcal{X}(t))$ for $t := s \Delta t$, $s = 0, 1, \ldots, N_T$.

elements.algorithm-2411-003: \caption{Solve $u$ by simulating Bessel processes in VSM} \label{gmm} Input: $n = $ the number of capitalization processes in the market, $m =$ the dimension of the time-changed Bessel processes to model $X_i(t)$ for $i = 1, \ldots, n$, $N_T = $ the number of uniform meshes on $[0,T]$, $n_p =$ sample trajectories used for Monte Carlo simulation of $u(\cdot)$. For $s = 0$ to $N_T$: \begin{enumerate} \item Initialize $k = 0$, the states $\mathcal{X}(s) := (x_1, \ldots, x_n)$, $\theta_0 = s$. \item While $\theta_k \leq T$: \begin{enumerate} \item Set $k \gets k+1$, $t_k := s + k \Delta t$. \item Generate $n_p$ samples of $m$-dimensional independent Brownian Motion $W(t_k)$. \item Simulate $n_p$ independent samples of $mn$-dimensional squared Bessel processes $Y(t_{k}) = \sum_{i=1}^n Y_i(t_{k}),$ where $Y_i(t_{k})$ is defined in \eqref{eq:yi} for $i = 1, \ldots, n$. \item Update $\theta_{k+1} = \theta_k + \frac{4}{Y(t_{k})} \Delta t$, where $\Delta t := T / N_T$. \end{enumerate} \item Evaluate $X_{i}(T)$ using interpolation techniques between the non-uniform mesh points $(\theta_k, \theta_{k+1})$. Then, compute $u(T-s, \mathcal{X}_s)$ by the Monte Carlo estimation \eqref{eq:mc-u}. \end{enumerate} Output: The optimal arbitrage path $u(T-t, \mathcal{X}(t))$ for $t := s \Delta t$, $s = 0, 1, \ldots, N_T$.
